title: crataegus albicans var. tatnalliana
intro: Crataegus albicans var. tatnalliana, also known as the Tatnall's hawthorn, is a variety of the Crataegus albicans tree, a type of flowering plant in the rose family native to eastern North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various heart and cardiovascular issues, including high blood pressure, angina, and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Crataegus albicans var. tatnalliana are attributed to its flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood circulation, and protect against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Crataegus albicans var. tatnalliana typically involve the leaves, flowers, and fruits being dried and made into teas, capsules, or tinctures for consumption.
